command.borislib.batching_chat_packets_b: When true, chat-related packets bypass the buffer for instant delivery.
command.borislib.batching_coalesce_bundl: Maximum number of sub-packets per BundlePacket. Vanilla client enforces a hard limit of 4096.
command.borislib.batching_coalesce_packe: List of packet class names to coalesce into BundlePackets (e.g. particles, sounds). Supports inner classes with dot syntax (Foo.Bar).
command.borislib.batching_enabled.commen: Master toggle for the smart packet batching system. When true, outgoing packets are buffered and flushed in batches instead of individually.
command.borislib.batching_flush_interval: Interval in milliseconds for the INTERVAL batching mode.
command.borislib.batching_ignored_packet: List of packet class names that should always bypass the buffer entirely.
command.borislib.batching_instant_packet: List of packet class names that should flush the buffer immediately when sent.
command.borislib.batching_max_batch_byte: Maximum bytes to buffer before forcing a flush.
command.borislib.batching_max_batch_size: Maximum number of packets to buffer before forcing a flush.
command.borislib.batching_mode.comment: Batching mode: smart_execution (flush on tick + limits), strict_tick (flush only on tick end), interval (flush on a timer).
command.borislib.batching_off_thread_byp: When true, packets sent from off the server thread bypass the buffer.
command.borislib.batching_packet_coalesc: When true, packets in the coalesce list are bundled together into BundlePackets on flush, reducing the client's received packet count.
command.borislib.batching_safety_margin_: Safety margin in bytes subtracted from the max batch bytes limit to prevent overflow.
command.borislib.batching_write_queue.co: When true, buffered packets are queued and written in a single event loop task instead of calling Connection.send() per packet, eliminating per-packet lambda allocations and cross-thread task scheduling.
command.borislib.condition.add.details_f: [id=%1$s, value=%2$s, duration=%3$s, op=%4$s, particles=%5$s, stacking=%6$s, persistent=%7$s]
[id=%1$s, value=%2$s, duration=%3$s, op=%4$s, particles=%5$s, stacking=%6$s, persistent=%7$s, inflictedBy=%8$s]
value=%1$s, duration=%2$s, timer=%3$s, op=%4$s, stacking=%5$s, particles=%6$s, persistent=%7$s, inflictedBy=%8$s
command.borislib.condition.not_living_en: Target must be a living entity
command.borislib.metrics_enabled.comment: When true, the metrics collection system is active and computes network statistics.
command.borislib.metrics_module_cpu.comm: Enables the CPU metrics module (process CPU usage estimation).
command.borislib.metrics_module_memory.c: Enables the memory metrics module (saved allocation estimation).
Enables the network metrics module (PPS, bandwidth).
command.borislib.metrics_module_write_qu: Enables the write queue metrics module (event loop task savings tracking).
command.borislib.metrics_update_interval: How often (in seconds) the metrics system recomputes derived statistics.
command.borislib.netstats.unknown_type: Unknown stats type: %s. Use: network, cpu, ram, all
command.borislib.opt_explosions_block_ch: Number of block changes in a single chunk required to trigger a full chunk resend instead of individual updates.
When true, block updates from explosions are grouped by chunk and replaced with full chunk resends if they exceed the threshold.
command.borislib.opt_explosions_log_opti: When true, logs a message each time the explosion optimization replaces block updates with a chunk resend.
command.borislib.testmod_features_enable: When true, the test commands showing what BorisLib can do will be enabled for operators.

gui.borislib.prev_page_sub: to go to the previous page

testmod.borislib.marker_remove_none: Found no markers in this world of that name

text.borislib.must_be_executed_by_player: This must be executed by a player
